_notes: Human-reviewed 2026-07-19, replacing the 1-rule Stage-4 auto output. Context: the original scrape MISSED Articles II-IV entirely (Municode serves those oversized chunk groups with Content=null on every doc; fixed in the scraper by per-doc fallback fetches), so the auto-classifier never saw the residential or commercial/office/industrial zones. After the re-scrape (481 -> 897 sources) the pack has all 13 articles. Rules are first-match and key on article_contains (breadcrumb[1], 2-level breadcrumbs 'Zoning' > 'ARTICLE ...') plus numeric-prefix title_contains overrides inside Article IV -- interior section numbers never exceed x09 within each hundreds block, so '4-12'/'4-13'/'4-14' uniquely select the 4-1200/4-1300/4-1400 blocks (scoped by article_contains so 11-412-style refs elsewhere cannot false-match). Zone identities from the Article III/IV/V zone-header sections (Sec. 3-100 R-20 ... Sec. 5-600 CDD) cross-checked against the city's live ArcGIS zoning layer (maps.alexandriava.gov/arcgis/rest/services/alxZoningWm/MapServer/1, field ZONING: R20/R12/R8/R5/R2-5/RA/RB/RCX/RC/RD/RM/RS(absent)/RT/RMF, CL/CC/CSL/CG/CD/CDX/CR/OC/OCM(50)/OCM(100)/OCH/I/UT/NR, CRMU/L-M-H-X, W-1, CDD #1-#31, KR/POS/WPR). Mapping rationale: (1) ARTICLE III -> residential-low-density: all 14 zones (R-20 down to RMF) are residential; the usual all-residential collapse per lynchburg/salem/staunton precedent covers the multi-unit RA/RCX/RC/RD zones. (2) ARTICLE IV overrides: 4-1200 block (I/Industrial zone) -> industrial-zone; 4-1300 block (UT/Utilities and transportation -- rail yards, transit facilities, heavy infrastructure) -> industrial-zone; 4-1400 block (NR/Neighborhood retail, Arlandria) -> mixed-use-core per the danville N-C / staunton B-1 neighborhood-retail precedent. (3) ARTICLE IV fallback -> commercial-employment: CL/CC/CSL/CG/CD/CD-X/CR commercial zones plus OC/OCM(50)/OCM(100)/OCH office zones (dwellings appear in some permitted-use lists but these are employment-first corridor/downtown commercial zones, the staunton B-3 style call). (4) ARTICLE V -> mixed-use-core (kept from the auto rules): CRMU-L/M/H/X, W-1 waterfront mixed use, CDD coordinated development. Deliberately left UNMAPPED (fall through to unknown/general): ARTICLE VI special/overlay zones (KR King Street retail, POS parks, UOS/WPR etc. -- niche or non-development), Articles I-II (general regs, definitions), and Articles VII-XIII (supplemental standards, parking, signs, historic, procedures, nonconformity, environmental).
